Title: More than 1,000 foreigners screened at hentian Klang during 'Op Mega'
Article:
KLANG: More than 1,000 foreigners were screened by the Immigration Department during "Op Mega" around the Hentian Klang area today, in an effort to combat the presence of illegal immigrants in the country.
The surprise inspection, which began at around 12pm, targeted shoplots, various business premises, and hostels identified as major focal points for the settlements and economic activities of the foreign community in the Klang district.
A survey by Bernama found that enforcement teams scrutinised the travel document status of each detained individual to ensure there were no violations under the Immigration Act 1959/63, including offences such as overstaying and lacking valid documents.
It is understood that the Immigration Department placed full focus on the screening process to identify any involvement of external parties or employers harboring illegal immigrants - including nationals from Nepal, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Pakistan, and Indonesia - at the inspected business premises.
The integrated operation also uncovered illegal activities at the involved premises when authorities found evidence of immoral activities and the sale of prohibited goods in several of the temporary accommodation units.
The large-scale proactive action was successfully carried out by 250 enforcement officers and personnel, resulting from strategic cooperation between Immigration Department's from Putrajaya and Selangor, the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM), and the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) Selangor.
All those detained were taken to the Selangor Immigration Department office for further investigation, and full details will be revealed tomorrow during a press conference by Immigration Department Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban in Putrajaya. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran newspaper lists Trump, European leaders as revenge targets
Article:
TEHERAN: A conservative newspaper in Iran known for its provocative tone has published a list of people to be targeted as revenge for the killing of supreme leader Ali Khamenei, including the leaders of the US, Israel and European countries.
US-Israeli strikes killed the late ayatollah on Feb 28, the first day of the Middle East war, and his son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei has vowed revenge.
"Vengeance is the will of our nation and must inevitably be carried out," Mojtaba said on Saturday in his first message since his father's funeral this week.
"These criminals, whose names appear on a list, will take to their graves the wish of a peaceful death in their beds."
The Hamshahri newspaper, which is published by authorities in the Iranian capital, released an infographic online late Saturday featuring photos of 13 foreign leaders alongside Mojtaba's statement.
The supreme leader in his message said Iran had compiled a list of individuals to be targeted, but did not offer names, and there was no suggestion the newspaper's list was officially endorsed.
The infographic includes US President Donald Trump, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, among others.
It does not appear in the paper's Sunday print edition.
Also on the list are US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth, French President Emmanuel Macron, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz.
During the war, Iran accused European countries of failing to condemn attacks on its territory, and of being complicit by allowing US military aircraft to fly through their airspace.
Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since before the war, and was reportedly wounded in the strikes that killed his father.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Form Five student hospitalised after suspected poisoning in school
Article:
SUNGAI SIPUT: A Form Five student was rushed to hospital after he was believed to have ingested a poisonous substance within the compound of a school here on Friday.
District police chief Superintendent A.W. Charan said police received a report on the incident the same day.
"The student was taken to hospital for treatment and is currently in stable condition.
"Initial investigations found no elements of criminal wrongdoing in the incident," he said in a statement.
Charan urged the public not to spread unverified information or speculation about the case.
He also called on the public to respect the student's privacy and refrain from interfering with police investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two men arrested, 2,000 yaba pills worth RM20,000 seized
Article:
KOTA BHARU: Police arrested two men and seized 2,000 pills suspected to be yaba, worth approximately RM20,000, in a raid at the parking area of Medan Selera Bukit Yong, Pasir Puteh, yesterday.
Pasir Puteh district police chief Supt Saari Yaacob said both suspects were arrested at 6.40pm by a team of officers and members of the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department of the Kelantan police contingent headquarters while they were in front of a Yamaha Y16ZR motorcycle without a registration number.
He said the inspection found 10 plastic packages containing 2,000 pills suspected to be yaba weighing 221.41 grammes.
"The estimated value of the seizure is RM20,000, and the initial urine screening tests on both suspects were found to be negative," he said in a statement today.
Saari said the police will apply for a seven-day remand order starting today to assist in the investigation, and the case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. - BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former ruler Sheikh Hamad is remembered as Qatar's moderniser
Article:
QATAR'S former emir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, a sweeping moderniser who seized power in 1995 and broke with tradition to hand it over to his son 18 years later, has died at the age of 74.
Qatar's top government body, the Amiri Diwan, announced that Sheikh Hamad had died on Sunday morning. It did not mention the cause of his death.
The Qatar that the current Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al-Thani inherited was already well on its way to being remade by his father.
Sheikh Hamad presided over one of the most consequential periods in the country's history, driving a rapid transformation of the tiny desert state that reshaped its economy, global profile and political ambition.
A forceful, independent-minded personality, he explained in a speech to mark his abdication in 2013 that he wanted a new generation "with their innovative ideas and active energies" to take over.
Sheikh Hamad was the architect of Qatar's effort to develop its liquefied natural gas (LNG) infrastructure that allowed it to get its vast gas reserves to global markets, turning the state into one of the world's largest exporters and laying the foundations for its vast wealth.
He established media network Al Jazeera, which gave Qatar an outsized voice in Arab politics and projected its influence far beyond the Gulf.
He also oversaw the successful bid to host the 2022 soccer World Cup, a move that firmly put Qatar on the global stage and accelerated a decade of infrastructure building that remade the capital Doha.
His foreign policy carved out a role for Qatar as a mediator, brokering talks in conflicts from Lebanon to Yemen and Darfur while maintaining ties with the United States — hosting US Central Command — as well as Iran and groups aligned with it.
That balancing act laid the groundwork for Qatar's current role in negotiations between the United States and Iran, and in its years-long efforts to halt the war in Gaza.
[[nid:1487092]]
Qatar under Sheikh Hamad played a prominent and controversial role during the 2011 Arab Spring uprisings, using its resources and outsized influence to back revolutionary movements and Islamist groups across the region.
While Doha portrayed its policy as support for popular demands for political change, critics accused it — and Sheikh Hamad — of selectively backing factions aligned with its interests, particularly groups linked to the Muslim Brotherhood.
This put Sheikh Hamad at odds with fellow Gulf monarchs in Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, which viewed many of these movements as a threat to regional stability and monarchical rule.
The activism elevated Qatar's regional profile but also deepened tensions with its neighbours and left a legacy that continues to shape Gulf politics.
Sheikh Hamad's abdication was intended to ensure a smooth succession and minimise discord within a ruling family with a long history of palace intrigue.
He himself seized power from his father in a bloodless coup in 1995.
A year later, he survived an attempted counter-coup that analysts attributed to his father, who had come to power in similar fashion in 1972 by ousting his cousin.
One of Sheikh Hamad's most consequential partners in Qatar's modernisation drive was one of his wives, Sheikha Moza bint Nasser, who fostered a public profile that was rare for a Gulf ruler's wife.
Sheikha Moza's influence rose alongside his efforts to reposition the state at home and abroad.
While Sheikh Hamad pushed through political and economic reforms that reshaped the country's trajectory, she advanced a parallel agenda in education, research and social development.
When the emir took power, he was the youngest leader in the region at 44.
He was viewed as less aloof than other Gulf Arab leaders, and could often be found at his favourite café in Doha's souq, talking with the patrons.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RE sector set for H2 2026 rebound on solar, battery storage demand
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's renewable energy (RE) sector is expected to regain momentum in the second half of 2026 as project activity accelerates, supported by a stronger pipeline, rising data centre demand and growing adoption of battery energy storage systems (BESS), said Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB).
The research house said major RE programmes, including the large-scale solar 6 (LSS6) and corporate renewable energy supply scheme (CRESS), are expected to require storage capabilities, prompting developers to bring forward battery procurement plans in the second half of 2026.
This is ahead of China's planned removal of export value-added tax (VAT) rebates on battery products from Jan 1, 2027.
HLIB expects the development to create a billion-ringgit opportunity for domestic players, particularly Solarvest Holdings Bhd and Samaiden Group Bhd, given their strong balance sheets and operating cash flows.
The firm maintained its "Overweight" rating on the sector, citing favourable structural growth drivers, an improving earnings cycle and potential regulatory improvements to CRESS.
It also maintained its "Buy" calls on Solarvest, with a target price of RM3.59, and Samaiden, with a target price of RM1.78, as it expects both companies to benefit from an extended order book upcycle driven by large-scale renewable energy projects and growing demand for solar-plus-storage solutions.
The firm said the rapid expansion of Malaysia's data centre pipeline would continue to be a key structural driver of renewable energy demand.
HLIB noted that engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract awards slowed in the first half of 2026 as developers delayed investment decisions amid geopolitical uncertainties and volatile upstream material prices.
However, it expects project activity to recover in the second half, supported by ongoing construction works, upcoming project deliveries and improving market visibility.
The firm said Malaysia remains on track to achieve its target of having 32 per cent of installed electricity generation capacity from renewable sources this year, providing a foundation towards the National Energy Transition Roadmap's (NETR) targets of 35 per cent by 2030 and 70 per cent by 2050.
On CRESS, the firm said slower-than-expected take-up, largely due to elevated and uncertain System Access Charges (SAC), continued to affect the scheme's commercial attractiveness.
It expects regulators to place greater emphasis on refining the CRESS mechanism in the second half of the year, including a possible rationalisation of SAC, which could serve as a key catalyst for the sector.
Meanwhile, rooftop solar adoption is expected to strengthen over the next six months, supported by the SuRIA Home rebate programme and the prospect of higher automatic fuel adjustment surcharges amid elevated fuel prices.
HLIB said applications under the Solar Access to the Grid Programme had exceeded 10,000 as of May, indicating improving market acceptance despite an initially slow uptake.
For solar equipment, the firm expects photovoltaic (PV) module prices to remain broadly stable through the end of 2026 despite China's removal of a nine per cent export value added tax rebate on PV products, which has pushed module prices up by between eight per cent and 9.6 per cent year to date.
It said persistent global oversupply and softer solar installation demand in China are likely to keep module prices within a range of US$0.11 to US$0.12 per watt over the near term.
HLIB also identified BESS as the sector's next major growth engine, following the Energy Commission's award of 400 megawatts (MW) and 1,600 megawatt hours (MWh) of storage capacity under the MyBeST programme, alongside Sabah Electricity's 100 MW and 400 MWh project in Lahad Datu.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Consumer spending seen slowing before year-end rebound: CIMB Securities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Consumer spending is expected to moderate in the third quarter of 2026 as festive-driven purchases seen earlier in the year taper off, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd.
In a note, the firm expects sales momentum to recover in the fourth quarter, supported by year end and festive spending, while government cash assistance and tourism continue to underpin demand for essential goods.
It added that sales momentum is likely to soften after spending was front-loaded into the first quarter, before recovering sequentially in the fourth quarter on the back of year-end and festive shopping.
It expects consumers to remain cautious amid inflationary pressures and macroeconomic uncertainties, with spending continuing to favour daily essentials such as food and household staples, while demand for discretionary products remains subdued.
CIMB Securities said government support would continue to cushion consumer spending in the second half of 2026, particularly for essential goods and products eligible under the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme.
Although the total allocation for the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sara programmes remains unchanged at RM15 billion this year, a larger proportion is now channelled through Sara, which the firm believes will support demand for essential consumer products.
On costs, CIMB Securities said consumer companies have largely avoided widespread shortages of key raw materials despite the earlier US-Iran tensions.
While lower crude oil prices and easing geopolitical risks could help reduce cost pressures, it expects higher costs to persist for plastic-based materials, logistics and utilities.
Given still fragile consumer demand, companies are likely to pass on higher costs gradually through selective average selling price increases, product mix optimisation and tighter cost controls rather than broad-based price hikes.
Retailers are also expected to step up promotional campaigns and value-focused offerings to defend market share as competition intensifies with continued store expansion across the sector.
CIMB Securities maintained its "Neutral" call on the consumer sector, noting that valuations already reflect softer consumer sentiment, weaker earnings momentum and persistent cost pressures, particularly among discretionary retailers.
However, it expects large-cap consumer staples to remain supported by resilient demand for essential goods, continued government cash assistance and spillover benefits from minimum wage increases.
The firm's preferred stocks are QL Resources Bhd, Empire Sushi Group Holdings Bhd and Life Water Bhd.
CIMB Securities said government cash transfers helped sustain retail spending during the Chinese New Year and Hari Raya festive periods, with five million eligible Malaysians receiving RM2.4 billion under the first two phases of STR in the first quarter, alongside the one-off RM100 Sara assistance for 22 million adults.
However, it expects the incremental boost to moderate in the second half of the year as the programme matures and retailers face higher comparison bases following broader Sara utilisation.
The firm also expects tourism to remain a positive driver for consumer spending, particularly for retailers and food and beverage operators with exposure to major cities, transport hubs and tourist destinations.
Malaysia received 10.6 million foreign tourists in the first quarter, with arrivals from China and Australia rising 25 per cent and 11 per cent year on year, respectively.
CIMB Securities said the government's target of attracting 47 million foreign visitors under Visit Malaysia Year 2026 should continue supporting selected retail and food and beverage (F&B) companies, although lingering geopolitical uncertainties and higher airfares remain downside risks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aeon Bank expands Shariah-compliant financing for businesses
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Bank (M) Bhd has expanded its business banking offerings with the launch of Term Financing-i and Working Capital Financing-i under its Aeon Bank Biz platform, as it seeks to strengthen access to Syariah-compliant financing for Malaysian businesses.
The two financing facilities are structured based on the Murabahah via Tawarruq concept, providing businesses with financing solutions for expansion and working capital requirements.
Term Financing-i is aimed at businesses undertaking medium- to long-term growth initiatives, including asset acquisitions and business expansion.
The facility offers financing without collateral, repayment tenures of up to 60 months and direct disbursement into customers' Aeon Bank Biz Business Current Account-i.
The bank said there are no application or utilisation fees.
Meanwhile, Working Capital Financing-i is designed to support businesses with short-term liquidity needs.
The facility provides a revolving credit line with multiple disbursement options within an approved financing limit, with financing tenures of up to three years and a disbursement period of up to 12 months.
Funds are managed digitally and credited directly into customers' business current accounts.
Beyond financing, Aeon Bank Biz offers digital banking services including payment and collection capabilities through DuitNow and Rentas, DuitNow QR settlements, and multi-user approval workflows for businesses.
The platform also includes the Biz Term Deposit-i, which offers profit rates of up to 3.20 per cent per annum on tenures starting from one day, with a minimum placement of RM500.
The bank said the expanded suite of financing products supports the broader push to improve financing access for small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
This is in line with Malaysia's target of increasing SMEs' contribution to gross domestic product to 50 per cent by 2030.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz sworn in for second term as Johor MB [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was today sworn in for a second consecutive term as Johor menteri besar.
The Machap assemblyman was reappointed after Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a commanding victory in the 16th Johor election to form the Johor government.
BN won 48 of the 56 seats contested, while Pakatan Harapan (PH) secured the remaining eight.
The swearing-in ceremony was held at Istana Bukit Serene here before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.
Also present were Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Dr Abdul Rahim Ramli and state secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir.
Earlier, Onn Hafiz arrived at Istana Bukit Serene at 3.40pm for the ceremony before Tunku Ismail arrived at 4.25pm.
The official menteri besar's vehicle carrying Onn Hafiz was seen leaving the palace at 4.50pm.
The Johor BN chairman lowered the vehicle's window and waved to members of the media, who had gathered outside the palace since 2pm to cover the ceremony.
This is the second time Onn Hafiz has been entrusted to lead Johor, having first been appointed the state's 19th menteri besar on March 15, 2022, following BN's victory in the 2022 Johor election.
He was sworn in then before Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, who is now the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, succeeding Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad as menteri besar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea asks North to help search for missing navy sailor
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea asked the North today to help search for and return a missing navy sailor believed to have drifted across the de facto inter-Korean maritime border.
The request follows reports that the sailor went missing this morning while a vessel was on patrol off South Korea's eastern coast near the Northern Limit Line (NLL).
"A sailor aboard a South Korean navy vessel conducting a security mission in the East Sea went missing on the morning of July 12 and is believed to have drifted north of the NLL," Seoul's Unification Ministry said in a brief statement provided to AFP, referring to the body of water also known as the Sea of Japan.
"As the South Korean navy is currently searching for the missing sailor, we request North Korea's cooperation, on humanitarian grounds, in the search and the individual's return," it added.
The sailor holds the rank  of seaman apprentice, Yonhap News Agency reported.
Defence Minister Ahn Gyu-back also instructed officials to "make every effort to rescue the missing serviceman as quickly and safely as possible", the defence ministry said in a statement, without providing further details.
The appeal comes as President Lee Jae Myung has sought to ease tensions with the nuclear-armed North by pursuing dialogue and confidence-building measures after years of strained relations.
Lee has taken a dovish approach to North Korea since taking office in June 2025, in stark contrast to his predecessor's hawkish policy.
Pyongyang, however, has not responded to Seoul's repeated overtures while deepening ties with Moscow. It recently hosted Chinese President Xi Jinping in its capital for a summit.
Official reports of the summit made no mention of denuclearisation. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lotus's Malaysia launches probe after pregnant woman is accused of shoplifting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lotus's Malaysia is conducting a thorough investigation into an incident in which a pregnant woman was accused of stealing at its supermarket in Selayang, Selangor, yesterday.
Lotus's Malaysia, in a statement to Harian Metro, said it was aware of the incident, which went viral on social media today.
"We are deeply sorry to learn that the matter has caused distress to the customer. We take the concerns raised very seriously.
"We are conducting a thorough investigation to obtain a full picture of what took place.
"We have also reached out to the customer to get a clearer understanding of her experience," it said.
It added that, as the investigation was ongoing, it would not be appropriate to provide any further comment at this time.
Earlier today, it was reported that a 38-week pregnant woman was
publicly accused of concealing stolen
merchandise in her baby bump by staff at a hypermarket in Selayang.
The husband, who recounted the incident on Threads, said staff asked for his wife's Buku Rekod Kesihatan Ibu, or maternity health booklet, and requested that she show her stomach to prove she was pregnant.
The couple were made to stand outside the cashier's area for nearly 20 minutes, surrounded by other shoppers, while staff attempted to contact a supervisor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India's Tata Consultancy Services plans up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers, seeks AI acquisitions
Article:
BENGALURU: Tata Consultancy Services is building a team of up to 8,900 forward deployed engineers and hunting for artificial intelligence (AI) acquisitions as it bets artificial intelligence will create new business rather than undermine outsourcing, two TCS executives told Reuters.
The strategy emerges amid investor concern that AI could disrupt India's US$315 billion IT services industry by reducing demand for engineering teams, shortening project timelines and squeezing prices as clients seek a share of productivity gains.
"We would be … ensuring that we have as many as 1 per cent to 1.5 per cent of our associates who could be what you would call FDEs," chief executive officer K Krithivasan said in an interview. TCS is India's largest software services firm.
Krithivasan's figures would translate to roughly 5,900 to 8,900 employees based on TCS's end-June headcount. Krithivasan did not say whether the company would hire externally or retrain existing staff.
Forward deployed engineers embed with clients to accelerate AI adoption and tailor tools to business needs, a role that has emerged as a hiring bright spot in a sector grappling with AI driven efficiency gains.
The plan pits TCS against firms such as OpenAI, Anthropic and Microsoft, which have expanded hiring for forward deployed engineers to help clients deploy AI tools.
The Mumbai based company is also evaluating acquisitions in AI, data security and cybersecurity, after largely shunning acquisitions for years and relying instead on organic growth until late 2025.
"We are looking at where we can find things which will help us enable or enhance our strategic positioning," chief financial officer Samir Seksaria said.
AI: FRIEND OR FOE?
Krithivasan dismissed concerns that AI would disrupt the outsourcing model, arguing that companies still need partners such as TCS to integrate and deploy AI systems.
"What you need is a deep knowledge of the customer environment to make it work. That is where we differentiate ourselves. This has nothing to do with cost arbitrage. It's essentially because of the talent pool that we have built," Krithivasan said.
Companies increasingly use multiple AI models and require partners such as TCS to connect those models with existing systems and manage data flows, he said.
Even so, TCS's annualised AI revenue growth slowed to 13 per cent in the first quarter from 28 per cent in the previous quarter. Krithivasan said he would like the business to grow about 25 per cent quarter on quarter over the long term but that he did not expect a linear trajectory.
TCS spends about US$1 billion annually on talent development and making AI accessible internally, with a focus on training, targeted hiring and niche recruitment in AI native technologies, Seksaria said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL allocates RM9.6mil to upgrade PPR Desa Tun Razak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: More than 8,000 residents of Program Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) Desa Tun Razak will benefit from RM9.624 million worth of refurbishment works to improve their living environment.
Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said in a statement today that the allocation would cover repainting works, fire safety and electrical system upgrades, as well as general maintenance involving 1,824 housing units.
"Of the total allocation, RM7.043 million has been set aside for repainting the buildings, while RM1.68 million will be used to upgrade fire prevention and electrical systems, including fire protection equipment, alarm systems and main switchboards.
"Another RM900,000 has been allocated for general maintenance works, including road repairs, replacement of street lighting and improvements to public facilities," he said.
[[nid:1487069]]
Fadlun said the upgrading project reflected DBKL's commitment to ensuring public housing remained safe, comfortable and well maintained.
"Every project undertaken must produce results that residents can see and experience. Our focus is not merely on completing the works on schedule but on ensuring they meet the required quality standards and provide long-term benefits," he said.
He added that DBKL would closely monitor the implementation of the projects to ensure they were completed according to specifications, on schedule and in compliance with safety and quality standards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Student arrested in alleged Cyberjaya prostitution raid
Article:
CYBERJAYA: A university student's bid to earn extra cash during her semester break ended with her arrest after police raided an apartment unit allegedly used for prostitution activities here yesterday.
The woman, believed to be in her 30s, claimed she was pursuing a double degree in Indonesia and had taken up the activity to earn extra money to support her studies.
"Yes, I have two degrees, yet I'm still foolish. What can I do? I had just started doing this and I was already caught.
"I took up this gig to earn extra money to finance my studies. I used to work at a factory but it got tiring and I earned a lot less.
"I would earn at least RM100 per customer and I would take up no more than three clients a day," she said after being detained by police.
The woman was among eight foreign women — three Thais and five Indonesians — and a local man arrested during Op Noda conducted by the Selangor police Criminal Investigation Department Anti-Vice, Gaming and Secret Societies Division (D7).
The operation, carried out following intelligence gathering and surveillance, targeted hotel rooms and condominium units allegedly being used for freelance prostitution activities in Cyberjaya, Damansara and Puchong.
Police also detained a local man in his 30s who is believed to have acted as a transporter after he was allegedly caught sending one of the women to meet a customer.
The women allegedly sourced customers through the WeChat and WhatsApp applications, charging between RM150 and RM540 an hour for sexual services.
Some of the women are believed to have entered Malaysia using social visit passes before overstaying.
According to sources, the women would meet customers at apartment or condominium lobbies before escorting them to rented units where the sexual services were allegedly carried out.
Police seized several items during the operation, including mobile phones, condoms, towels, access cards and cash.
All those detained were taken to the Petaling Jaya, Sepang and Serdang district police headquarters for further investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 372B of the Penal Code, Section 56(1)(i) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963, and Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family sues ECRL-linked parties over alleged home safety risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A family has filed a RM1.33 million lawsuit against three parties involved in the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) project, alleging that construction works have compromised the safety and structural integrity of their home.
Hishamuddin Ahmad and his wife, Faizah Abdul Jalil, filed the writ of summons at the High Court here, naming project contractor China Communications Construction (ECRL) Sdn Bhd (CCC), project owner Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL), and the Selayang Municipal Council (MPS) as defendants.
The plaintiffs claimed construction activities on a nearby hillslope since early 2024 had caused significant water accumulation in the soil, leading to structural tremors.
"Our house shook like an earthquake," Hishamuddin said, adding that the project was being "built on a hillslope that we found was not properly supervised."
He alleged that residents were not given prior notice before heavy works began, and that a dilapidation survey was only conducted in February 2024 following repeated complaints.
"This case isn't just about physical damage, it is about the safety of my family, expert reports have shown a real and ongoing safety risk, rather than merely cosmetic damage," he said.
Hishamuddin said the defendants were uncooperative when the family requested the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and Site Investigation reports in March 2024.
He alleged that CCC and MRL told the family the documents were in the "public domain" and that they had to "find them independently".
[[nid:1487059]]
The plaintiffs also claimed that letters sent to the Ministry of Transport, Department of Environment, Prime Minister's Office and MRL chief executive officer Datuk Darwis Razak received no response.
"Unfortunately, the MPS president and MRL's CEO never visited the site to see the situation for themselves," he said.
Frustrated by the lack of response, Hishamuddin engaged a private geologist in August 2024, whose report found high water saturation in the slope.
While MPS ordered a full study in early 2025, MRL reportedly said in March 2025 that existing studies were sufficient.
A subsequent soil study commissioned by the family concluded that the "possibility of the slope collapsing is very high".
Despite a Letter of Demand issued in October 2025, the defendants reportedly refused to settle.
The writ was officially filed on April 24, 2026.
Hishamuddin said representatives from MPS and CCC visited his home on the day of the filing and admitted there was water accumulation, while promising remedial action.
"But for me, it's already too late because the legal process has already begun," he said.
He said three other houses were also affected, but he was proceeding with legal action alone due to the high costs of independent technical studies.
He also expressed concerns over long-term risks once ECRL operations begin in 2027, citing his home's proximity to the Gombak Integrated Transport Terminal, about 500m away.
He cited previous Malaysian slope failure incidents, including Bukit Antarabangsa and Highland Towers, as examples of the risks involved.
The 665km ECRL project is scheduled to begin passenger services in 2027.
The New Straits Times has reached out to CCC, MRL and MPS for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi vows Bersama will fight on as Johor polls was 'only the beginning'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Bersama Malaysia president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has asserted that the party's mission remains far from over, saying its recent electoral debut was the "beginning of a journey" rather than a conclusion.
In a statement following the party's performance in the Johor election, he said that building a political movement to truly return power to the people would not happen overnight.
"This decision is not the end, it is the beginning... we know this journey will not be easy, we know building a movement that truly returns power to the people will not happen within 50 days.
"Yet, we keep moving forward, and you move forward with us," he said.
Reflecting on the party's rapid formation, he expressed his gratitude to the voters, volunteers, and contributors who supported the 50-day-old party.
"Every vote, every conversation, every rebuke, and every show of support is a trust we carry forward," he said, pledging that the party would learn from its weaknesses and take every lesson to heart.
Rafuzu said the party's struggle was born from a desire to introduce a new political culture, distinct from the "old ways" of established parties.
He urged supporters not to let disappointment or an "exhausting system" deter them from their goals.
"This struggle must not die just because we are disappointed, it must not be surrendered wholesale to those who see politics as a game of power.
"It must be carried forward together with a new generation and grounded in dreams and aspirations, not fear and threats," he said.
Recounting Bersama's evolution from a volunteer-led initiative to its arrival on the parliamentary stage, he said the experience provided vital insights into the brand of politics the party hopes to build.
"We have been through it all, from the volunteer stage at the very start when we had nothing, all the way to the parliamentary stage.
"It started from a desire to help, we felt there were things that could be done better," he said.
Looking ahead, he described the party's trajectory as a long-term commitment to honesty and public service.
"The seed has been planted, now, it is time for us to nurture it together, Bersama's journey has only just begun," he said.
Bersama contested the 16th Johor election as its maiden electoral test, and
lost all its deposits
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Back for term two: Onn Hafiz arrives at Istana Bukit Serene for MB swearing-in
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here this afternoon, where he is expected to be sworn in for a second term as Johor menteri besar.
Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, arrived in the official menteri besar's vehicle at 3.40pm.
He is expected to take the oath of office before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.
Earlier today, he had an audience with His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, at Istana Pasir Pelangi.
Also present was BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Yesterday, BN secured a commanding victory in the 16th Johor election, winning 48 of the 56 seats contested.
The remaining eight seats were won by Pakatan Harapan (PH).
Onn Hafiz polled 20,382 votes to defeat PH candidate Nur Hafiz Roslan by a majority of 15,375 votes, retaining the Machap seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman killed in hit-and-run crash on KL-Seremban Highway
Article:
KAJANG: Police are tracking down the driver of a white car who fled after crashing into a Perodua Bezza in a fatal accident on the Kuala Lumpur-Seremban Highway early today.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the accident occurred at Km298.8 of the highway about 5.45am.
"Initial investigations found that the brown Perodua Bezza, carrying a driver and two passengers, is believed to gone out of control before veering to the right, crashing into the road barrier and spinning.
"A white car travelling behind then collided with the Bezza before fleeing the scene," he said.
He said a 27-year-old woman, who was a passenger in the Bezza, died at the scene.
"The driver, a 20-year-old man, and another male passenger, aged 22, sustained injuries and are receiving treatment in the Red and Yellow zones of Serdang Hospital," he said in a statement.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death byy reckless or dangerous driving.
The section carries a punishment of between two and 10 years' jail and a fine of between RM5,000 and RM20,000 upon conviction.
Police have appealed to anyone with information on the crash or the white car involved to contact investigating officer Inspector Ahmad Zahrein Muhd Suod at 017-3818422.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global energy market deteriorating due to higher competition – Eni chief
Article:
ROME: The situation on the global energy market could worsen in the short term amid declining global oil reserves and increased competition for energy resources, Claudio Descalzi, the chief executive officer of Italian oil and gas company Eni, believes.
"In the short term, this is possible. Competition is destined to intensify due to the gradual decline in oil reserves and the resumption of purchases by Asian countries. At the same time, Europe will need additional supplies of American liquefied natural gas to replenish underground storage facilities for the winter," Descalzi said in an interview with Il Sole 24 Ore newspaper, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti.
According to the top manager, European countries will need to purchase approximately 35 billion cubic metres of gas to bring their storage capacity to the target of 80 per cent.
"Suffice it to say that the European storage capacity, excluding Italy, is currently around 47 per cent, while in our country it has already reached 70 per cent," he said.
Descalzi also noted that global oil inventories are rapidly declining. According to him, since the start of the conflict in the Persian Gulf, they have been declining by an average of 3.8 million barrels per day, and in May, the rate of decline accelerated to 4.6 million barrels per day, exceeding the levels seen in 2021 during the COVID-19 pandemic.
On Wednesday night, the US military launched a series of powerful strikes against Iran. US Central Command claimed that this was in response to Iranian actions against commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. The Iranian military claimed retaliatory strikes on US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, with Iranian authorities accusing Washington of violating the ceasefire agreement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ten of 34 women candidates secure seats in Johor state election sweep
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Ten of the 34 women candidates who contested in the 16th Johor election won seats in the State Legislative Assembly, underscoring continued confidence in women's leadership in the state's political landscape.
Of the total, seven candidates were from Barisan Nasional (BN), while three represented Pakatan Harapan (PH).
The seven victorious BN candidates are Nadhirah Afiqah Abdull Rahim, who won the Serom seat, Nor Rashidah Ramli (Parit Raja), Alwiyah Talib (Endau), Norlizah Noh (Johor Lama), Fauziah Misri (Penawar), Chan San San (Johor Jaya) and Hasrunizah Hassan (Pulai Sebatang).
PH's successful women candidates were Felicia Poh Rui Ling in Penggaram, Chu Poh Yee in Mengkibol and Kartiyaini Jeyapalan in Skudai.
Ledang Puteri Umno chief Nadhirah Afiqah, who was making her electoral debut, retained the Serom seat for BN after securing a majority of 9,406 votes against Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Mahfidz Omar and Ahmad Nazari Abd Hamid of PH.
Nor Rashidah's victory in Parit Raja reflected stronger support for BN after she secured 19,572 votes for a majority of 13,576 in a three-cornered contest against PH candidate Shazwan Dzainal Abidin and Dr Mohamed Maliki Mohamed Rapiee of PN, compared with a majority of 4,219 votes in the 2022 Johor election.
Alwiyah, meanwhile, completed a hat-trick by retaining Endau for a third term, winning by a majority of 3,041 votes in a four-cornered contest against PN candidate Hasnul Hakimi Hussien, Jati Awang of the Malaysian Orang Asli Party (ASLI) and Saiful Nizam Samat of PH.
Norlizah retained Johor Lama with a commanding majority of 16,344 votes in a three-cornered contest against PN candidate Aisah Esa and the election's youngest candidate, Danish Hossman Abd Rahman of PH.
Fauziah also secured a landslide victory in Penawar with a majority of 15,776 votes in a three-cornered contest involving candidates from BN, PH and PN.
Chan pulled off one of the election's biggest upsets by capturing Johor Jaya, long regarded as a DAP stronghold. She secured 35,971 votes to defeat candidates from PH, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) and an Independent.
Hasrunizah also retained Pulai Sebatang after defeating Haniff @ Ghazali Hosman of PH in a straight fight with a majority of 13,590 votes, an increase of 6,325 votes compared with the 2022 Johor election.
Meanwhile, PH's Penggaram candidate Poh, 28, recorded her maiden election victory as she retained the seat for the coalition, defeating BN's Boo Chin Liong by a majority of 4,137 votes in a straight fight.
Chu retained Mengkibol for PH with a majority of 4,213 votes after defeating BN's Yap Zhi Peng.
Skudai-born lawyer Kartiyaini retained the traditional PH seat with a majority of 15,280 votes in a four-cornered contest against Tan Hiang Kee of BN, Eugene Chua Meng Chong of Bersama and Amir Syafiq Ameer Soekre of Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM).
The 34 women candidates accounted for nearly 20 per cent of the total field, compared with 138 male candidates.
BN won 48 of the 56 seats contested in the election, with the remaining eight going to PH.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar extends condolences to Qatari emir, people over leader's death
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended Malaysia's deepest condolences to the government and people of Qatar following the passing of its former leader, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani who died today at the age of 74.
"On behalf of Malaysia, I extend my deepest condolences to His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, the Qatari royal family, and the people of Qatar on the passing of His Highness the Father Amir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani," he said in a statement.
"During his 18 years as a leader, Sheikh Hamad transformed Qatar into a nation of global significance."
Anwar added that under Sheikh Hamad's leadership, Qatar achieved remarkable economic progress and emerged as an influential voice in regional and international affairs.
He described Sheikh Hamad's 2013 decision to hand over leadership to a new generation as a rare act of statesmanship, calling the late leader "a steadfast friend of Malaysia and a respected figure across the Muslim world".
"May Allah SWT bless his soul and grant strength and comfort to the royal family and people of Qatar in this time of grief. Al-Fatihah," he said.
Sheikh Hamad ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, when he abdicated in favour of his son, the current Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.
His death was
announced
on Sunday by Qatar's official state office.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested after GOF chase uncovers RM813,000 in suspected smuggled cigarettes
Article:
KOTA BARU: A 29-year-old man was arrested with 400,000 sticks of suspected smuggled cigarettes worth RM813,000 after a chase at Kampung Pulau Panjang here last night.
General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was made by a team from the brigade headquarters' Intelligence Branch.
"Following a tip-off, the team spotted two suspected vehicles — a Proton Exora and a Nissan Grand Livina — at Kampung Pauh about 9pm.
"They then decided to follow the vehicles until they stopped at Kampung Pulau Panjang.
"The two drivers fled into nearby bushes, but GOF personnel managed to apprehend the driver of the Proton Exora after a brief struggle, while the other suspect escaped.
"The arrested suspect sustained minor injuries to both legs and his right hand during the scuffle," he said.
Radzi said checks on both vehicles uncovered 40 large packages containing 400,000 sticks of white cigarettes believed to be smuggled items.
He said the cigarettes were believed to have been destined for the market in Kuala Besut, Terengganu.
"Initial investigations revealed that the suspect was paid between RM150 and RM200 for each delivery.
"He claimed he collected the vehicle, already loaded with the cigarettes, by the roadside in Kampung Pauh and dealt with the syndicate only through the 'Buzz' application.
"He also claimed he did not know the identity of the mastermind," Radzi said.
He added that further inspection found the Proton Exora's chassis number did not match the registration plate displayed, with the vehicle's actual registration.
"We also suspect both vehicles were using false registration numbers," he added.
The case has been referred to the Kota Baru district police station for further investigation under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 and Section 108(3)(f) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Undocumented migrants scatter as Immigration raids Klang Bus Stand
Article:
KLANG: Undocumented foreigners ran in every direction, with some even jumping out of buses, when the Immigration Department raided the Klang Bus Stand here this afternoon.
The surprise operation, which began at about noon, triggered chaotic scenes as scores of foreigners attempted to flee or hide after spotting enforcement officers.
The area, long known as a popular weekend gathering spot for migrant workers, was cordoned off by enforcement personnel before the raid began, preventing many from escaping.
The integrated operation was conducted by the Selangor Immigration Department together with the National Registration Department, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, and the Putrajaya Immigration Department.
More than 1,000 foreigners of various nationalities were screened to verify whether they possessed valid travel documents and work passes.
The New Straits Times observed several undocumented migrants scrambling into nearby shoplots and alleyways in a desperate attempt to evade arrest, while others fled on foot.
However, enforcement officers, who had cordoned off the vicinity in advance, quickly intercepted many of those attempting to escape.
A trader, who declined to be named, said the commercial area regularly attracts large numbers of foreign nationals, particularly on weekends.
"They gather here to socialise and shop for goods from their home countries.
"Some also litter the area by spitting betel quid residue along the five-foot ways, leaving the place dirty and foul-smelling," he said.
The area is frequented mainly by Bangladeshi, Myanmar and Nepalese nationals.
The Immigration Department is expected to hold a press conference tomorrow, during which the director-general will announce details of the operation, including the number of arrests made.
Those detained are being investigated under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying, and Section 6(1)(c) of the same Act for not possessing valid travel documents or passes.
During checks today, officers found a stall operated by an undocumented foreigner allegedly selling a crystal substance believed to be methamphetamine, along with betel leaf and contraband cigarettes.
The foreigner operating the stall is believed to have fled after noticing authorities cordoning off the area.
In a subsequent raid on a nearby hotel, officers found signs that it had allegedly been operating as a brothel.
They discovered lubricant, condoms and used tissues in one of the rooms. The women believed to have been working there had reportedly fled before officers entered the premises.
However, one foreign man was found locked inside a room and was escorted out in handcuffs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sungai Perak turns bluish again, probe underway
Article:
IPOH: The state government will await findings from the Mineral and Geoscience Department (JMG) and Department of Environment (DOE) before deciding on further action after Sungai Perak in Kampung Bukit Amarikan, Gerik, turned bluish again.
Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said both agencies had been deployed to the site to conduct investigations and collect water samples, particularly to determine whether aquatic life in the area remained safe for consumption.
He said the reported fish deaths must first be investigated to establish whether they were caused by contamination or other factors, including breathing difficulties caused by sediment or dust blocking the fish gills.
"Yesterday (Saturday), JMG together with DOE went to the site to conduct studies and collect water samples following reports of fish deaths.
"The cause of the deaths needs to be determined, whether it was due to poisoning or because the fish could not breathe as the river water was filled with mud or dust that blocked their gills.
"I have contacted the District Officer and JMG. However, from a technical standpoint, only JMG has the authority to provide comments on the river water composition," he said.
Saarani was speaking to reporters after officiating at the key handover ceremony for the Rumah Perakku Astana Chemor Phase 1 housing project here today.
The Perak Housing and Property Board project, developed by an appointed developer, comprises 184 units, with 114 units under Phase 1 and 70 open-market homes under Phase 2.
Saarani said similar incidents had occurred previously, including in 2019, and were often linked to mineral deposits in Hulu Perak, particularly during periods of heavy rain.
He said follow-up action similar to measures taken after Sungai Perak near Kampung Sungai Papan Bridge, Gerik, turned bluish in October last year would be considered, depending on the latest findings by JMG and DOE.
"Of course, action will be taken because this has happened repeatedly. As in 2025, the water eventually returned to normal after some time, but this time it has turned blue again.
"Therefore, another study will be conducted to determine the actual source of the bluish colour," he said.
NST had previously reported that Sungai Perak near Kampung Bukit Amarikan, Gerik, had turned greenish-blue, with the incident suspected to be linked to possible contamination.
Several visuals showing the unusual colour change, along with dead fish at the location, had since gone viral.
The earlier incident near Kampung Sungai Papan Bridge in October 2025 was later confirmed by the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability to have involved the presence of heavy metals or certain chemicals, which may have originated from mining activities.
Following the incident, the
state government ordered three mining companies in Hulu Perak to temporarily halt operations
. The companies were later allowed to resume operations on Jan 13.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Son of Belgian wildfire victim disputes Spanish officials' account of warnings
Article:
THE son of a Belgian man who perished in Spanish wildfires  disputed authorities' claims that his father and other victims ignored official advice to shelter in place, saying the emergency services gave them no guidance.
Belgian virologist Thomas-Wolf Verdonckt told Reuters on Saturday that he spoke to his father, 63-year-old businessman Stanislas Verdonckt, by phone just before 9 pm (1900 GMT) on Thursday evening as the fire advanced on the mountain village of Bedar in Spain's southeastern Almeria province.
Stanislas Verdonckt was among eight victims of the wind-whipped wildfire found dead in a valley below the Paraje el Curato area where he lived on the outskirts of Bedar, according to his 33-year-old son.
Verdonckt, who lives in Belgium, travelled to Spain after the fire and spoke with surviving neighbours.
No officials told the group that the fire was heading their way or that it would be safer for them to stay at home than flee, Verdonckt said.
"The people who died did not fail to follow any orders because no orders were given. No information was provided," he said.
"They only started to run when the flames were almost upon them. That was their absolute last resort."
Authorities have said local officials and police went door-to-door or telephoned residents with instructions on how to evacuate safely or to shelter in place, depending on how the fast-moving fire was advancing in their area.
Bedar Mayor Angel Collado said he urged the group including Stanislas Verdonckt to shelter in place.
The Andalusian regional government, which oversees emergency services and local administration, and the Spanish Civil Guard police, which was involved in rescue efforts, did not immediately respond to requests for comment on Verdonckt's account.
In total, 12 people — mainly foreigners like Stanislas Verdonckt and one Spaniard — died trying to escape the wildfires as they reached Bedar, which is above the town of Los Gallardos.
[[nid:1487023]]
Their identities have not been officially confirmed, and firefighters are still struggling to contain the blazes.
A group of neighbours including his father first tried to drive away on Thursday night by a paved road, but they were beaten back by flames, Verdonckt said.
"They couldn't get through via the main road because they were not warned in advance. Nobody told them that the fire was coming from that direction, and when they tried to get out, it was too late," he said.
The group then tried to flee by car in the other direction on the neighbours' dead-end dirt lane, which hugs a mountainside. But they could not make it out, abandoned their cars and sought to escape on foot, he said.
"It was not a choice. They drove to the end of the trail, and when even that was in flames, some people chose to run and try to get into the valley," he said.
A neighbour who survived in his home told Verdonckt that the flames approached close enough to the house to touch, he said.
Verdonckt said his father, a keen hiker and photographer, had a home in the area for many years, knew the terrain well and spoke Spanish.
During their final phone conversation, Verdonckt said his father debated the options to protect himself.
Stanislas Verdonckt was always calm "even in the most desperate situations" and went over his "plan A, B and C", Verdonckt said.
"My father is one of the smartest people I know. He's always very analytical and was just checking boxes: 'Can we do this? Can we do that?'," he said.
"At that point, it was just minutes before they were engulfed and they were trapped."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing hunter found dead wrapped in canvas; two friends held
Article:
SIPITANG: A man reported missing after going fishing and hunting in the Kampung Takula forest on July 3 was found dead, his body wrapped in canvas, following the arrest of two friends believed to be involved.
Police believe the victim was accidentally shot while hunting.
His body was found in a forested area about 1km from Kampung Takula after police questioned the two suspects on Friday.
District police chief Superintendent Awang anak Suand said the case was initially classified as a missing persons case before investigations led to the arrests.
"Questioning of the two suspects led to the discovery of the victim's body, which had been wrapped in canvas.
"Initial investigations found that the suspects allegedly wrapped the body because they panicked after the incident," he said when contacted today.
Police also seized two homemade shotguns, locally known as bakakuk, from the suspects yesterday to assist in the investigation.
"The two suspects, both local men in their 50s, have been remanded to assist investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
"Further investigations are ongoing to determine the actual cause and circumstances of the incident," he said.
The victim entered the Kampung Takula forest with the two men on July 3 for a fishing and hunting trip.
The two companions returned on July 5 without him, prompting his family to lodge a missing persons report on July 8.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Joss paper factory badly damaged in Rawang fire
Article:
RAWANG: A fire tore through a joss paper factory in Taman Industri Rawang this afternoon, destroying about 80 per cent of the building.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the department received an emergency call at 1.01pm reporting the blaze along Jalan Batu Arang, Sungai Bakau.
He said firefighters from the Rawang fire station arrived six minutes later and found the fire engulfing a Class B factory measuring about 200 by 300 feet.
"The fire destroyed an estimated 80 per cent of the building. The blaze was brought under control about 35 minutes after firefighting operations began.
"There were no casualties reported," he said in a statement today.
Ashrul said 20 firefighters from the Rawang, Batu Arang, Bukit Sentosa, Selayang and Damansara fire stations were deployed to the scene.
He said the operation involved a Fire Rescue Tender, a Light Fire Rescue Tender, two water tankers and an emergency medical vehicle.
"Firefighters are using water from two water tankers to extinguish the blaze, and firefighting operations are still ongoing," he said.
The cause of the fire and estimated losses have yet to be determined.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South China Sea joint statement says China's maritime claims have no basis
Article:
TOKYO: China's expansive maritime claims in the South China Sea have no legal basis, Japan, the Philippines, the United States and 11 other countries said in a joint statement on Sunday to mark the 10th anniversary of a landmark international tribunal ruling.
The Philippines won the 2016 case at the Permanent Court of Arbitration that found China's sweeping claim of sovereignty in the South China Sea had no basis under international law, a decision that Beijing continuously rejects.
・"We reaffirm that the award rendered ten years ago by the Arbitral Tribunal is a significant milestone and is final, legally binding, and definitive between China and the Philippines," the joint statement said.
・The Philippines and China have been involved in a series of maritime confrontations in recent years, with Manila accusing Beijing of "dangerous manoeuvres" inside its exclusive economic zone, including the use of water cannons to interfere with resupply missions to Philippine-held features.
・Besides Japan, the Philippines and the United States, parties to the joint statement include Australia, Britain, Canada, Estonia, Germany, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, New Zealand, Romania and Slovenia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pivotal US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71
Article:
WASHINGTON: US Senator Lindsey Graham passed away on Saturday evening after a brief and sudden illness, the communications director for his office said in a post on X early on Sunday.
Graham, 71, was a prominent Republican senator from South Carolina. Earlier in his career, he was a vocal critic of US President Donald Trump before becoming one of his most loyal allies on Capitol Hill.
"Senator Graham's family appreciates prayers at this time and asks for privacy during this incredibly difficult period," his office added.
Graham was elected to the US Senate in 2002.
Before serving in the upper house, he was elected to the US House of Representatives in 1994 for South Carolina's third congressional district, according to his website.
A defence hawk, his website said he had "consistently pushed for outcomes in the War on Terror that protect our long-term national security interests."
Graham recently served as chairman of the Senate Budget Committee. Graham also served as a member of the Senate Committee on Appropriations, Senate Judiciary Committee and Senate Committee on Environment and Public Works.
Graham was not married and lived in Seneca, South Carolina.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Victory in Johor no guarantee of GE16 success, says BN's Saarani
Article:
IPOH: Barisan Nasional's landslide victory in the Johor election should not be seen as a definitive sign that the coalition will triumph in the 16th General Election (GE16).
Perak BN chief Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said past elections showed that voter sentiment could shift rapidly.
He cited BN's victories in the Melaka election in 2021 and the Johor election in 2022, which were followed by a different outcome in the last general election.
Saarani, who is also Perak menteri besar, said while the coalition welcomed and appreciated the support from Johor voters, it could not afford to take victory for granted.
"We can't say that this is a sign of what will happen in GE16," Saarani told reporters after handing over keys to owners of the Rumah Perakku Astana Chemor Phase 1 project.
"Voters' preferences can change, although we hope their support will remain.
"We do not know what may happen because people's sentiment can be influenced by various factors, including the cost of living, floods and other issues that will continue to arise," he said when asked if the outcome of the Johor polls signalled a return of support to BN ahead of GE16.
BN secured a landslide victory in the Johor election,
winning 48 of the 56 state seats
up for grabs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz arrives at Istana Bukit Serene for Johor MB swearing‑in ceremony
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here this afternoon, believed to be to attend the swearing-in ceremony as Johor Menteri Besar.
The vehicle carrying the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman was seen entering the palace grounds at about 3.40pm, with members of the media gathered since noon to cover the ceremony.
Earlier, the vehicle carrying Onn Hafiz was seen leaving the Menteri Besar’s official residence in Saujana at 3.35pm.
The swearing-in ceremony for the Johor Menteri Besar is expected to take place at the palace this afternoon.
Earlier, the palace, through an invitation issued to the media, said coverage of the ceremony would only be permitted at the Mahkota gate of Istana Bukit Serene.
The area around the palace entrance also saw the presence of security personnel, government officials and media representatives awaiting the arrival of dignitaries and developments related to the ceremony.
The appointment of the new Johor Menteri Besar follows Barisan Nasional’s (BN) landslide victory in the 16th Johor State Election yesterday, after winning 48 of the 56 state assembly seats. — Bernama

Title: Johor MB swearing‑in: Media gather at Bukit Serene as palace keeps appointee’s name secret
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — A Johor Menteri Besar is expected to be sworn in at Istana Bukit Serene here at 3pm today.
According to an invitation circulated via WhatsApp, the Johor Palace announced that the swearing-in ceremony would take place at the palace this afternoon.
“Members of the media may cover the event from the Crown Arch (Pintu Mahkota) of Istana Bukit Serene. The swearing-in of the Johor Menteri Besar is scheduled for 3pm today, July 12,” the invitation stated.
However, the invitation did not disclose the name of the individual who would be appointed as Menteri Besar.
Media personnel began gathering since noon at the Menteri Besar’s official residence in Saujana and outside the gates of Istana Bukit Serene to monitor the latest developments.
Earlier today, Sultan Ibrahim Iskandar granted an audience to Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi at Istana Pasir Pelangi, according to a post on His Majesty’s official Facebook page.
The meeting, which lasted nearly an hour, was also attended by Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
BN secured a landslide victory in the Johor state election yesterday, winning 48 of the 56 contested state seats. — Bernama

Title: Teen escapes after Myvi plunges into Bachok river following suspected drowsy driving
Article:
BACHOK, July 12 — A 16-year-old student escaped after the Perodua Myvi he was driving plunged into a river near the Pengkalan Petah water gate here yesterday, after he is believed to have fallen asleep while driving.
Bachok Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Nor Azini Che Wil said the Form Four student from a school in Kota Bharu was travelling home after attending tuition classes when the incident occurred at about 3.10pm.
Sinar Harian
reported that the teenager told rescuers that he had dozed off due to fatigue and only realised the vehicle had plunged into the river, which was estimated to be about 15 metres deep, after he woke up.
“The teenager used his left arm to break the front passenger-side window to allow himself to escape from the vehicle.
“He was then assisted by members of the public using a rope to climb back up to the riverbank,” she was quoted as saying.
Nor Azini said the vehicle had plunged into the river close to the bank, allowing the teenager to remain calm during the incident.
She added that the student suffered an injury to his left arm and was taken to the Hospital Pakar Universiti Sains Malaysia (HPUSM) for further treatment.
The fire department received an emergency call at about 3.20pm, with the operation fully concluded at 5pm.

Title: Johor polls disappointment for Pakatan, but Loke vows fresh push ahead of Negeri Sembilan election
Article:
JELEBU, July 12 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) respects the decision of voters in the 16th Johor State Election which was won by Barisan Nasional (BN) yesterday and accepts the outcome as part of the democratic process.
PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke said the coalition had anticipated a tough contest given the current momentum of support for the state government. Nevertheless, PH managed to secure eight out of the 56 state seats contested.
Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, said the party successfully defended six out of the 10 seats it won in the previous state election, proving that DAP continues to enjoy a solid support base, particularly in urban areas.
“The pattern of support for PH remains intact. All six seats were won with majorities exceeding 50 percent,” he told reporters after launching the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) Kampung Angkat Madani programme at Kampung Chennah here today.
In yesterday’s election, BN secured a two-thirds majority after winning 48 seats.
Loke said the shift to straight fights had also led to a transfer of votes that favoured BN, giving the coalition an advantage in several seats previously won through three-cornered contests.
“Of course, this result in Johor is disappointing for PH. However, every day is a new beginning. We respect the decision of the Johor electorate because that is their right in a democratic system,” he said.
He said the Johor election result should not be viewed as an absolute gauge, as each state has a different political landscape, issues and dynamics.
The Chennah assemblyman said PH was now focused on defending its existing seats and strengthening public support ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election.
“The political dynamics in Negeri Sembilan are different because PH is the incumbent state government. Our focus is to defend the seats won and regain the people’s mandate to continue forming the state government.
“In the last Negeri Sembilan state election, PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14. Therefore, we still have a strong foundation to defend our existing seats,” he said.
Loke said all PH candidates must work harder to retain the coalition’s 17 seats in Negeri Sembilan, while striving to win more constituencies. — Bernama

Title: Nga Kor Ming stands by pledge to resign over Najib issue as Puad challenges him to quit Cabinet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming today stood by his earlier pledge to resign from the Cabinet if former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak were released without serving an appropriate sentence, as former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi challenged him to make good on his words.
In a statement on his Facebook, Nga also congratulated Barisan Nasional (BN) on its landslide victory in the Johor state election, saying the coalition’s two-thirds majority came with a heavy responsibility to govern with integrity.
“The democratic process must be respected by all parties,” he said, adding that the mandate given to BN should be carried out with integrity and accountability.
Nga also thanked voters who supported Pakatan Harapan (PH), as well as the coalition’s election workers and volunteers for their efforts throughout the campaign.
“Although we did not win this time, tomorrow is another day.
“Pakatan Harapan remains committed to continue working hard and serving the people and the nation,” he said.
Addressing calls for his resignation following remarks made during an exclusive interview with Oriental Daily, Nga said his comments reflected his long-held position as an advocate and solicitor of the High Court of Malaya that the integrity of Malaysia’s judiciary must be defended.
He said that if Najib, whom he said had left Malaysians burdened with RM51.4 billion in debts linked to 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), were released without serving a punishment commensurate with his conviction, it would undermine the credibility of the country’s judicial system and the rule of law as enshrined in the Rukun Negara.
“If such a situation were to occur, for the sake of defending the integrity of our beloved judicial system, I am willing to resign as a member of the Cabinet.
“This has nothing to do with personal matters. It is a question of principle. I remain steadfast in upholding this principle,” he said.
Shortly after Nga’s statement, former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi called on the DAP vice-chairman to resign immediately instead of offering further explanations.
“I want to advise Nga Kor Ming to resign immediately as Housing and Local Government Minister. Don’t give so many excuses. Protect your integrity, your party and the Unity Government,” Puad said in a Facebook post.
Puad said he had honoured his own principles when he left his party despite doing so when it was in a strong position, claiming his message had been delivered even though he lost his position on the Umno Supreme Council.
He also took a swipe at Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh, saying Nga should not emulate him.
“Don’t follow Akmal’s behaviour. He said he wanted to resign but didn’t. He gave all kinds of reasons. Where there’s a will, there’s a way; where there isn’t, there are a thousand excuses. The point is, he has no integrity,” Puad said.

Title: Youth vs veteran in Johor polls: Felicia Poh, 28, wins Penggaram; Samsolbari, 65, holds Semarang
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — At just 28, Felicia Poh Rui Ling became the youngest successful candidate in the 16th Johor state election, reflecting voters’ confidence in young leadership after winning the Penggaram state seat and retaining it for Pakatan Harapan (PH).
The youngest DAP candidate in the election polled 24,522 votes to defeat Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Boo Chin Leong, 65, who secured 20,385 votes, giving her a majority of 4,137 votes.
Penggaram, which has 70,294 registered voters, is one of three state constituencies under the Batu Pahat parliamentary seat.
Poh’s victory ensured Penggaram remained under PH after the incumbent, Gan Peck Cheng, chose not to defend the seat.
Meanwhile, Datuk Samsolbari Jamali, 65, emerged as the oldest successful candidate in the election after retaining the Semarang seat for a sixth consecutive term.
The Ayer Hitam Umno division chief defeated Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Muhammad Syafiq Abdul Aziz and PH candidate Ramli Abd Hamid with a commanding majority of 14,679 votes.
Muhammad Syafiq and Ramli received 2,695 and 2,205 votes, respectively.
Samsolbari has represented Semarang since first winning the seat in 2004.
The youngest candidate in the election was Danish Hossman Abd Rahman, 23, who contested the Johor Lama seat under the PH banner, while the oldest candidate was Lim Chin Eng, also known as Roland Lim, 73, who represented PN in Stulang.
A total of 172 candidates contested the 56 seats in the Johor state election.
For the latest news on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Policeman killed after Perodua Kelisa veers off Pasir Puteh‑Kota Bharu road, thrown from vehicle
Article:
KOTA BHARU, July 12 — A policeman was killed after being thrown out of the car he was driving when the vehicle is believed to have lost control and veered off the road at Kilometre 30 of the Pasir Puteh-Kota Bharu Federal Road near Kampung Banir Belikong junction in Pasir Puteh last night.
Pasir Puteh police chief Supt Saari Yaacob said in the 11.50pm incident, the 28-year-old victim, who was attached to the Setiu district police headquarters (IPD) in Terengganu, was believed to have been on his way home to Pasir Puteh from Kota Bharu.
He said preliminary investigations found that the Perodua Kelisa driven by the victim lost control before veering off the road upon reaching the scene.
“As a result of the crash, the victim was thrown from the vehicle and suffered serious head injuries before being pronounced dead at the scene,” he said in a statement today.
Saari said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, and further investigations are underway to determine the cause of the crash.
He urged anyone with information on the incident to contact Pasir Puteh district police traffic investigating officer Insp Abdul Munir Mohd Fauzi at 013-9872579 or the nearest police station to assist in the investigation. — Bernama

Title: Selangor JPN vows strict action in Rawang bullying case, pledges full cooperation with police
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 12 — The Selangor State Education Department (JPN) is extending full cooperation in the investigation into an alleged bullying incident involving several secondary school students in Rawang, which gained public attention after going viral on social media.
In a statement, the Selangor JPN said no individual involved would be protected and that strict action would be taken against those found responsible in accordance with existing regulations.
“The Selangor JPN takes serious note of the recent video circulating on social media regarding an incident involving student misconduct at a school in Rawang.
“The matter is currently being looked into by the police. The Selangor JPN will not compromise on any form of misconduct that could that could affect students’ safety and well-being,” the statement said.
The department also urged the public to respect the investigation process being conducted by the authorities. — Bernama

Title: Home Ministry to review EAIC report, vows stricter SOPs and vetting in citizenship approvals
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 12 — The Home Affairs Ministry (KDN) will review the findings and recommendations submitted by the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) together with relevant agencies, including the National Registration Department (JPN), the Immigration Department (JIM) and the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), to identify areas for improvement in the management of citizenship applications through naturalisation.
In a statement today, KDN said the review would focus on strengthening standard operating procedures (SOP), documentation, coordination of work processes and governance mechanisms in line with public service best practices.
It said continuous training would also be provided to officers and staff involved to ensure they possess the necessary knowledge and skills.
“KDN wishes to emphasise that the power to consider citizenship applications is subject to the provisions of the Federal Constitution and is exercised with due caution, taking into account various factors, including public interest, national security and the facts of each case.
“KDN will also extend full cooperation to any agency with the jurisdiction to conduct further investigations should there be matters requiring legal action,” the ministry said.
Citizenship applications under Article 19 of the Federal Constitution are processed through a comprehensive assessment of each application based constitutional provisions, relevant laws and the interests of national security and sovereignty, it said.
“KDN also obtains scrutiny and vetting from various related agencies according to their respective jurisdictions before any application is considered,” it added.
The ministry said it remained committed to strengthening the integrity, transparency and efficiency of its delivery to ensure all citizenship-related matters are handled professionally and with accountability.
“KDN takes note of the EAIC report and consistently respects the check-and-balance function of institutions established under the law, and will examine every recommnedation submitted to strengthen the ministry’s service delivery system and governance,” it said.
